evals.name: new-notebook-from-scratch
evals.prompt: Make me a quick notebook in /tmp/eval_work/ called sales_explore.ipynb that loads /tmp/eval_work/sales.csv (columns: region, month, product, revenue) and makes two plots: monthly revenue totals broken down by region as a bar chart, and the top 10 products by total revenue as a horizontal bar. Save the PNGs to a figures/ subfolder next to the notebook. Exploratory — I'll be tweaking the plots interactively.
evals.expected_output: A .ipynb that uses %matplotlib widget, calls fig.savefig before plt.show, never calls plt.close after plt.show

evals.name: retrofit-inline-notebook
evals.prompt: I want to make the plots in /tmp/eval_work/inline_analysis.ipynb interactive instead of static. Update the notebook so I can zoom/pan in the figures from Lab. Don't change the analysis logic, just the plotting setup.
evals.expected_output: Notebook's %matplotlib inline is changed to %matplotlib widget, AND every plt.close(fig) that immediately follows plt.show() is removed

evals.name: add-cell-to-widget-notebook
evals.prompt: Add a new cell at the end of /tmp/eval_work/widget_model_eval.ipynb that plots a histogram of the residuals (df.y_true - df.y_pred) with 30 bins. Save it to figures/residuals_hist.png.
evals.expected_output: Notebook gains a new code cell that follows widget conventions: savefig before show, no plt.close after plt.show, doesn't break the existing %matplotlib widget setup
